citation: CORRECTED 2026-07-13 (was 8,885, from Wikipedia's "Iranian rial" article transcription of Central Bank of Iran "Annual Review 2013/14"): see data/raw/iran-cbi-imf-fx-verification-2004-2010/manifest.json. New value is the average of the WDI/IMF-IFS official rate for Iran, 2004 (8,613.99, already used independently in data/charts/fx__official_vs_parallel_gap_irn/data.csv) and an IMF Article IV fiscal-year calendar-blend (~8,616) built from real quarterly data in IMF Country Reports 04/307 Table 48 and 06/129 Table 47.
notes: Moderate confidence (not high): no CBI daily-rate primary archive reaches back to 2004 (cbi.ir's own 'Exchange Rates Statistics' page is only Wayback-archived from Dec 2005 onward). Corroborated by Bahmani-Oskooee's own Aug 15, 2004 personal spot observation (see the point_observation row immediately below: 8,712-8,775 across buying/selling/informal-market rates), which sits close to the new estimate and well below the superseded 8,885 figure. The old 8,885 figure is not fabricated -- it is CBI's own compiled annual value per Wikipedia's transcription -- but this round's independent, converging evidence points 2-3% lower.

citation: Bahmani-Oskooee, Mohsen (2005), "History of the Rial and Foreign Exchange Policy in Iran," Iranian Economic Review, Vol. 10, No. 14 (Fall 2005), Table 4, p.16-19. https://ier.ut.ac.ir/article_30891.html (PDF: https://ier.ut.ac.ir/article_30891_cc9816769f0293f06d92344e31426eea.pdf) (author's personal first-hand observation, main text p.16, footnote 1)
notes: Author Bahmani-Oskooee's own personal observation of the dollar BUYING rate at an authorized bank (Bank Melli) in Iran on Aug 15, 2004. Same-day SELLING rate was 8,724 and the rate outside authorized banks (i.e. the informal/negotiated market) was 8,775 rials/dollar -- all three within 0.7% of each other, consistent with the paper's point that by 2004 the free-market and negotiated/authorized-bank rates had converged. This single dated spot observation extends real, directly-cited coverage 8 months past the source table's own Dec-2003 cutoff.

citation: CORRECTED 2026-07-13 (was 9,408, from Wikipedia's "Iranian rial" article transcription of Central Bank of Iran "Annual Review 2013/14"): see data/raw/iran-cbi-imf-fx-verification-2004-2010/manifest.json. New value is the average of 3 independent primary/near-primary sources that agree within 0.1% of each other: (1) 9 dated CBI-primary daily rate observations (cbi.ir/ExRates via Wayback Machine) spanning the full 2007 calendar year, averaging 9,274; (2) IMF Article IV fiscal-year 2007/08 period average of 9,285, identically reproduced across THREE independent IMF report vintages (Country Reports 08/284, 10/74, 11/241); (3) this project's own existing WDI/IMF-IFS official-rate series for Iran, 2007 (9,281.15, already in data/charts/fx__official_vs_parallel_gap_irn/data.csv).
notes: High confidence -- the superseded 9,408 figure EXCEEDED every single one of the 9 daily rates CBI itself published in 2007 (the observed maximum across the full year was 9,325, in Oct-Nov); the old figure was not internally consistent with any observed data point.

citation: CORRECTED 2026-07-13 (was 9,143, from Wikipedia's "Iranian rial" article transcription of Central Bank of Iran "Annual Review 2013/14"): see data/raw/iran-cbi-imf-fx-verification-2004-2010/manifest.json. New value is the average of 2 independent primary/near-primary sources that agree within 0.2% of each other: (1) 12 dated CBI-primary daily rate observations (cbi.ir/ExRates via Wayback Machine) spanning the full 2008 calendar year, averaging 9,413; (2) this project's own existing WDI/IMF-IFS official-rate series for Iran, 2008 (9,428.53, already in data/charts/fx__official_vs_parallel_gap_irn/data.csv).
notes: High confidence -- the 2008 CBI daily-rate series captures both the Mar-Apr low (~8,957-9,021, peak oil-boom rial strength) and the sharp Sep-Dec spike (9,642-10,124, global financial crisis); the superseded 9,143 figure sat below nearly the entire observed range. The old 9,143 figure had also produced an anomalous NEGATIVE 3.03% 'parallel below official' premium in data/charts/fx__official_vs_parallel_gap_irn/data.csv -- the only negative value across the whole 2003-2010 genuinely-unified-rate window, and the technical symptom that first flagged this year as suspect.
